title: Frosch
content:

{{Quote|And for the final task, you must be brave to steal the eggs from a frosch's cave.|Logray explaining the fungus cure|<ref name="TSD" />}}
'''Frosch''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Lizard/Legends|lizard]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], a [[Warrick family/Legends|family]] of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] on Endor needed to acquire frosch [[Egg/Legends|eggs]] for a [[Rokna tree fungus potion|potion]] to save their [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Deej Warrick]], from [[rokna tree fungus]]. One of Warrick's [[Gender/Legends|sons]], [[Weechee Warrick|Weechee]], went into the [[frosch grotto]] to try and get the eggs; he was successful doing so but had to quickly escape the [[Cave/Legends|cave]] as several frosch chased after him. He barely escaped, falling down a [[Water/Legends|waterfall]] to avoid the frosch and surviving thanks to his brothers' help.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Such greedy lizards.|Willy chides the frosch|<ref name="TSD" />}}
[[File:Frosch-TSD.png|thumb|right|300px|Three frosch in their grotto.]]
The frosch were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of six-[[Limb/Legends|legged]], [[Color/Legends|orange]],<ref name="TSD" /> [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]]<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Lizard/Legends|lizard]] who lived in [[Cave/Legends|caves]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. They had three-toed feet at the end of each limb, red [[Tongue/Legends|tongues]], yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a long tail,<ref name="TSD" /> and they were known for their sharp,<ref name="CSWE" /> jagged [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]<ref name="Weechee">{{DB|character|weechee|Warrick, Weechee}}</ref> that could devour anything.<ref name="WATDW">''[[Wicket and the Dandelion Warriors: An Ewok Adventure]]''</ref> The skin on their chin and around their eyes was gray as opposed to the orange of the rest of their bodies. Frosch laid [[Egg/Legends|eggs]] that were small and white in color.<ref name="TSD" />

==Behavior==
{{Quote|I am on an important mission. And all you can think about is your stomachs.|Willy scolds the frosch|<ref name="TSD" />}}
Frosch were quick to attack intruders who went after their eggs and were willing to try and eat [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]<ref name="TSD" /> as they were [[Diet/Legends|carnivores]]. Frosch were known for having short tempers.<ref name="CSWE" />

==History==
{{Quote|Hmm! You should be ashamed of yourself. The way you frosch are carrying on, you'd think Ewoks are good to eat.|Willy admonishes the frosch|<ref name="TSD" />}}
[[File:FroschWeechee-TSD.png|thumb|left|300px|Weechee tries to escape the frosch.]]
Many frosch on Endor lived in the [[frosch grotto]], a cave whose opening was a large [[Water/Legends|waterfall]].<ref name="TSD" /> The grotto was within the [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]] and had a deep pond.<ref name="WATDW" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{EwoksDate|the appearance of the frosch|[[To Save Deej]]}}</ref> the Ewok [[Deej Warrick]] was gravely injured by a [[rokna tree]]'s [[Rokna tree fungus|fungus]]. His [[Warrick family/Legends|family]] brought him to their [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|village's]] [[Shaman/Legends|medicine man]], [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], who prescribed a [[Rokna tree fungus potion|potion]] requiring frosch eggs to save his life<ref name="TSD" /> as frosch eggs were a healing-potion ingredient.<ref name="CSWE" /> He warned that the frosch eggs would require the most bravery of the ingredients needed to be gathered;<ref name="TSD" /> everyone was scared of the frosch.<ref name="WATDW" /> Warrick's [[Gender/Legends|sons]]—[[Weechee Warrick|Weechee]], [[Widdle Warrick/Legends|Willy]], and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]]—split up to get the different ingredients. Weechee trekked to the frosch grotto on the directions of Logray, entering alone. He tiptoed inside, trying to avoid the frosch, but in the attempt, he got many frosch eggs stuck to his clothing, attracting three growling frosch' attention. Weechee pocketed a few of the eggs and backed out of the cave near the top of the waterfall, where a fourth frosch arrived, cornering him. He tried to run away but was unable to gain traction with the slippery ground,<ref name="TSD" /> eventually saying a prayer<ref name="WATDW" /> and jumping into the waterfall.<ref name="TSD" />

The Ewok was able to survive the fall by hanging on to a branch protruding from the waterfall, but he became stuck there and began shouting for help as the frosch eyed him. It was then that the rest of the Ewoks arrived, extending a rope for him to hold on to in order to escape. Weechee successfully swung to a tree and landed back on the ground, but the frosch followed, cornering the Ewoks and their [[Gupin/Legends|Gupin]] companion, [[Mring-Mring]], up against the side of a [[Stone/Legends|stone]] formation. They were all forced to try and scale it, but Willy slipped down.<ref name="TSD" />

[[File:EwoksEscapeFrosch-TSD.png|thumb|right|300px|The Ewoks and Mring-Mring are chased by the frosch.]]
The frosch—whose numbers had by then increased to over a dozen—approached him, snarling, but Willy stood up and began lecturing them on the importance of his mission and how greedy it was that all they could do was think about their [[Stomach/Legends|stomachs]]. They stepped back slightly as he further told them that it was shameful to think Ewoks a good thing to eat; however, the frosch were unconvinced by Willy's words and crept closer to him, prompting the Ewok to race up the side of the formation. He joined the rest of the party at the top, realizing that they were trapped as the frosch encircled them. The frosch then also began climbing up the stone, so Mring-Mring quickly used his [[Magic/Legends|magical]] abilities to transform into a large winged creature and fly away while carrying the Ewoks.<ref name="TSD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The frosch first appeared in "[[To Save Deej]]," the fourth episode of the [[Ewoks: Season One|first season]] of the animated series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']],<ref name="TSD" /> which first aired on [[September 28]], [[1985]].<ref name="I27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref> The [[2001]] [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 1|first issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' is the lone source to capitalize them as "Frosch," so this article assumes it erred.<ref name="FF1">{{FactFile|1|WIC 1-4|Wicket W. Warrick}}</ref> While the episode refers to the frosch as lizards,<ref name="TSD" /> the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry for Weechee Warrick called them [[Salamander/Legends|salamanders]].<ref name="Weechee" /> This article assumes that the Databank erred.

''[[Wicket and the Dandelion Warriors: An Ewok Adventure]]'', an adaptation of the episode written by [[Larry Weinberg]]<ref name="WATDW" /> and published in [[November]] 1985,<ref name="Amazon">{{BanthaCite|31|Ewoks & Droids: Read All About 'Em}}</ref> mentions that Weechee intentionally stuck his hand a rock to find the eggs and that when he found many of them stuck together, he separated a single egg to take. It also maintains that he went deeper into the frosch cave before jumping into the water.<ref name="WATDW" /> As the episode depicts him finding the eggs accidentally and fleeing the cave soon after the frosch find him,<ref name="TSD" /> this article uses that version of the events.
